title: Design System: Digital Space Spotlight Generator

display.purpose: Slide title only — the largest, most confident type because the slide is the hero.

headline.purpose: App header title; quiet brand presence.

body.purpose: All running text, inputs, editable fields; hints at 12px.

label.purpose: Functional form-group caps only — never a decorative eyebrow.

shadows.name: ambient-lift
shadows.purpose: The single resting elevation — panels, slides, primary buttons, thumbnails. Purple-tinted so even the shadow stays in family.

shadows.name: modal-lift
shadows.purpose: Deeper lift, only for the dialog card floating above the blurred scrim.

shadows.name: hover-lift
shadows.purpose: Slide hover elevation (token --shadow-lift), one step above ambient-lift.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Default transition on hover/focus/active for buttons, tabs, dropzones — fast, state-only, no choreography.

motion.name: spin
motion.purpose: Loading spinner rotation inside the primary button.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The Generate / Download action. Solid Wesley Purple, full-width, soft lift. (Retires the legacy violet-coral gradient.)
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { width: 100%; padding: 12px; border: 0; border-radius: 12px; font: inherit; font-weight: 700; color: #fff; background: #4F2759; box-shadow: 0 6px 24px rgba(60,40,90,0.10); cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s, filter 0.15s; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #3D1D45; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px #EFE7FF, 0 6px 24px rgba(60,40,90,0.10); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.55; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary actions (download, forget). White fill, hairline border, ink text shifting to purple on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { width: 100%; padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #E7E1EF; border-radius: 12px; font: inherit; font-weight: 700; color: #2B2536; background: #fff; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 0.15s, color 0.15s; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { border-color: #4F2759; color: #4F2759; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: #4F2759; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px #EFE7FF; } .ds-btn-ghost:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard field. White fill, hairline border, calm purple focus glow.
components.html: <input class="ds-input" type="text" placeholder="Paste the article text…" />
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; padding: 9px 11px; border: 1px solid #E7E1EF; border-radius: 10px; font: inherit; color: #2B2536; background: #fff; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.15s, box-shadow 0.15s; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #6B6478; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #4F2759; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px #EFE7FF; }

components.name: Mode Toggle
components.description: Segmented control in a lilac trough; active tab is a white pill with soft lift and purple text.
components.css: .ds-modes { display: flex; gap: 6px; background: #F6F2FB; padding: 5px; border-radius: 12px; } .ds-modes button { flex: 1; padding: 9px 6px; border: 0; border-radius: 9px; background: transparent; font: inherit; font-weight: 600; color: #6B6478; cursor: pointer; transition: 0.15s; } .ds-modes button:hover { color: #4F2759; } .ds-modes button.active { background: #fff; color: #4F2759; box-shadow: 0 6px 24px rgba(60,40,90,0.10); }

components.name: Metadata Chip
components.description: Pill carrying band / year / theme / routine on the title slide. Wesley Purple Soft on Wesley Purple text.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-block; background: #EFE7FF; color: #4F2759; padding: 4px 11px; border-radius: 999px; font: inherit; font-size: 12.5px; font-weight: 600; }

components.name: Spotlight Slide
components.description: The signature component: a white editable slide card with a solid Wesley Purple accent bar (migrated from the legacy gradient), kicker-free heading, and inline-editable title.
components.html: What is this image really asking us to notice?
components.css: .ds-slide { background: #fff; border: 1px solid #E7E1EF; border-radius: 14px; box-shadow: 0 6px 24px rgba(60,40,90,0.10); overflow: hidden; } .ds-slide-bar { height: 6px; background: #4F2759; } .ds-slide-body { padding: 18px 20px; } .ds-slide-title { margin: 0; font-size: 22px; font-weight: 800; line-height: 1.2; color: #2B2536; border-radius: 8px; padding: 6px 8px; transition: background 0.15s; } .ds-slide-title:hover { background: #FAF8FF; } .ds-slide-title:focus { background: #fff; outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px #EFE7FF; }

components.name: Info Alert
components.description: Calm status note using the soft-purple tint; the danger variant flips to a muted red wash.
components.html: Your API key is saved only in this browser.
components.css: .ds-alert-info { padding: 11px 13px; border-radius: 11px; font-size: 13px; line-height: 1.45; background: #EFE7FF; color: #3D1D45; border: 1px solid #DDD0FF; }

narrative.overview: A tool a teacher reaches for between classes, built to recede. Soft-lilac and white surfaces, one familiar humanist sans, and the only thing that earns visual weight is the teacher's own content. Because the output is AI-generated and student-facing, the system signals safety and control over cleverness. The canonical brand is Wesley Purple (#4F2759) + Wesley Gold (#C59F40) — the export palette; the live bright violet (#7C4DFF) and coral (#FF7043) with gradient treatments are legacy drift on a path to retirement. It rejects consumer-AI flash, the generic SaaS dashboard, gimmicky edtech, and the opposite failure of the cramped grey utility form.
keyCharacteristics: Single humanist sans (Segoe UI / system stack), one family across every role.
Soft-lilac canvas, white panels, gentle ambient lift — never flat-grey, never glassy.
One deep accent (Wesley Purple) doing the work; gold reserved for rare emphasis and the export.
Content is the hero: the four editable slides get the strongest contrast and the most room.
Trust signals everywhere: inline edit affordances, honest loading/error states, a clear Forget for the API key.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Wesley Purple is the only interactive accent. Selection, focus, primary action, and links all speak in it. Gold is emphasis, not interaction; coral is legacy.

rules.name: The Wesley Voice Rule
rules.body: The canonical brand is #4F2759 purple + #C59F40 gold — the export palette. The in-app bright violet (#7C4DFF) and coral (#FF7043) are legacy drift; new work uses the canonical pair and existing uses migrate toward it.

rules.name: The Weight-Not-Face Rule
rules.body: Hierarchy is built from weight (400 to 800) and size, never from a second font. One family, many weights.

rules.name: The No-Eyebrow Rule
rules.body: Uppercase tracked micro-labels are permitted only as functional form captions. The slide kicker is a decorative eyebrow tell and is being retired.

rules.name: The One-Shadow Rule
rules.body: There is exactly one resting elevation. Surfaces either sit flat or wear the Ambient Lift. The modal is the single exception.

dos: Do use Wesley Purple #4F2759 as the single interactive accent and reserve Gold #C59F40 for rare emphasis.
Do build hierarchy from weight and size in one sans family; let the teacher's slide content hold the strongest contrast.
Do keep one resting elevation (Ambient Lift 0 6px 24px rgba(60,40,90,0.10)); flat or lifted, nothing in between.
Do verify Muted (#6B6478) text hits at least 4.5:1 on the lilac canvas; bump small or critical text toward Ink.
Do keep trust signals visible: inline edit affordances, honest loading/error states, a clear Forget API key control.
Do make every interactive component show default, hover, focus-visible, active, and disabled for the keyboard-first audience.
donts: Don't ship consumer-AI flash — no glowing gradients, sparkle motifs, or AI theatrics. Retire the gradient primary button and gradient slide bar for solid Wesley Purple.
Don't drift to the generic SaaS dashboard — no metric-card grids, no console density.
Don't go gimmicky edtech — no mascots, badges, gamification, or over-rounded playful everything.
Don't swing to the cramped grey utility form; restraint is carried by space and soft lilac, not by draining colour.
Don't scaffold with decorative uppercase tracked eyebrows — the slide kicker is a tell.
Don't introduce a second interactive hue; the legacy bright violet and coral are drift, not a palette.
Don't use border-left/border-right greater than 1px as a coloured stripe (rework the .resp-note 2px edge).
